 {"id":"bd-uz8r","title":"Phase 2.3: TOON deletion tracking","description":"Implement deletion tracking in TOON format.

## Overview
Phase 2.2 switched storage to TOON format. Phase 2.3 adds deletion tracking in TOON format for propagating deletions across clones.

## Required Work

### 2.3.1 Deletion Tracking (TOON Format)
- [ ] Implement deletions.toon file (tracking deleted issue records)
- [ ] Add DeleteTracker struct to record deleted issue IDs and metadata
- [ ] Update bdt delete command to record in deletions.toon
- [ ] Design deletion record format (ID, timestamp, reason, hash)
- [ ] Implement auto-prune of old deletion records (configurable TTL)

### 2.3.2 Sync Propagation
- [ ] Load deletions.toon during import
- [ ] Remove deleted issues from local database when imported from remote
- [ ] Handle edge cases (delete same issue in multiple clones)
- [ ] Deletion ordering and conflict resolution

### 2.3.3 Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for deletion tracking
- [ ] Integration tests for deletion propagation
- [ ] Multi-clone deletion scenarios
- [ ] TTL expiration tests

## Success Criteria
- deletions.toon stores deletion records in TOON format
- Deletions propagate across clones via git sync
- Old records auto-prune after TTL
- All 70+ tests still passing
- bdt delete command works seamlessly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:23.722066816-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:27.491932-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:27.491932-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uz8r","depends_on_id":"bd-iic1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:37:23.726825771-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-v0x","title":"Auto-detect issue prefix from existing JSONL in 'bd init'","description":"When running `bd init` in a fresh clone with existing JSONL, it should auto-detect the issue prefix from the JSONL file instead of requiring `--prefix`.

Currently you must specify `--prefix ef` manually. But the JSONL file already contains issues like `ef-1it`, `ef-1jp` etc., so the prefix is known.

**Ideal UX**:
```
$ bd init
Detected issue prefix 'ef' from existing JSONL (38 issues).
✓ Database initialized...
```

This would make fresh clone hydration a single command: `bd init` with no flags.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:21.049215-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-02T17:11:19.751009583-05:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T21:57:11.164293-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-v29","title":"Deletions pruning doesn't include results in JSON output","description":"## Problem

When `bd compact --json` runs with deletions pruning, the prune results are silently discarded:

```go
// Only report if there were deletions to prune
if result.PrunedCount > 0 {
    if jsonOutput {
        // JSON output will be included in the main response
        return  // <-- BUG: results are NOT included anywhere
    }
    ...
}
```

## Location
`cmd/bd/compact.go:925-929`

## Impact
- JSON consumers don't know deletions were pruned
- No way to audit pruning via automation

## Fix
Return prune results and include in JSON output structure:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "compacted": {...},
  "deletions_pruned": {
    "count": 5,
    "retention_days": 7
  }
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:48:59.730979-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:11:54.363653-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:11:54.363653-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-v2mr","title":"Add RequiredSections() method to IssueType","description":"Add method to internal/types that returns recommended sections per issue type (bug, task, feature, epic). This is the foundation for validation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:06.129337-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2026-01-01T11:28:00.829607-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T11:28:00.829607-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented RequiredSections() method on IssueType with tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v2mr","depends_on_id":"bd-ou35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:22.697598-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip"}]}
